title: terminalia macrophylla
intro: Terminalia macrophylla, also known as the 'Big-leaf Combretum', is a tropical tree native to the Western Ghats of India and Sri Lanka. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Terminalia macrophylla have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various ailments, and the leaves can be consumed as a tea to reap its health benefits.
